• Irving Jaffee, Wikipedia – overview of life and Olympic achievements

8/8

Jaffee was later inducted into the US Skating Hall of Fame in 1940 and the International Jewish Sports Hall of Fame in 1979. His story reminds us that Olympic history is about talent, identity, and resilience, not just medals.
7/8
US skater Maxim Naumov makes emotional Olympic debut one year after losing parents in plane crash

During the Great Depression, he pawned his Olympic medals to help support his family, but the pawn shop later closed and he never got them back. He continued in the sport through exhibitions, coaching, and winter sports programs.


6/8

Jaffee’s perseverance paid off. He won two Olympic gold medals at Lake Placid in the 5,000m and 10,000m events, becoming one of the US’s most successful athletes and proving that excellence can thrive despite discrimination.


5/8

At the 1932 Winter Olympics in Lake Placid, NY, Jaffee excelled despite hostile treatment. Accounts describe antisemitism and harassment from some teammates and members of the community, including signs reflecting prejudice against Jews.

You encounter a man with 13,000 jars of marinara sauce, in a recursive chain of red wagons pulling each other. He says his only dream in life is to swim in an Olympic pool of marinara. 

Anything less and his life has failed, but he is overjoyed, as he finally has enough sauce.

With the move of the Olympic Stadium to Flushing Meadows, the International Broadcast Center/Main Press Center was also moved from Manhattan to Willets Point. While the IBC/MPC wasn't built, Willets Point was still redeveloped, with an MLS stadium opening in 2027 for New York City FC.

Citi Field: However the stadium fell apart after NY State declined to approve 400 million in funding. Scrambling, NYC made an agreement with the Mets to build a stadium that could be converted to baseball from an Olympic Stadium. Citi Field simply became a baseball stadium after the bid's failure.

Hudson Yards: NYC used the bid to spearhead the redevelopment of Hudson Yards, with West Side Stadium as its anchor. Meant as the Olympic Stadium and the new Jets stadium. This includes the renovation of the Javits Center (multiple sports), the High Line, and the extension of the 7 to Hudson Yards

Hunters Point South: While the Olympic Village wasn't built at LIC's Hunters Point South, the site was still redeveloped for mixed-use. Including 11 acres of waterfront park, 5K residential units, and a school with 52 classrooms, gym, auditorium, library, science labs, and cafeteria.

Barclays Center: The arena was conceived by Bruce Ratner after purchasing the then NJ Nets in 2004 to move them to Brooklyn to be the anchor for a development. The arena was proposed to be the venue for artistic/trampolining gymnastics and basketball. Barclays Center eventually opened in 2012.

NYC Ferry: The bid proposed a system of private ferries that could take athletes, the media, etc to and from the various sites such as the Olympic Village, Breezy Point for sailing, and Staten Island). The East River Ferry’s pilot program launched in 2011 and morphed into the current NYC Ferry.

Davis has set nine world records during his career and held the top spot on the world Adelskalender (all-time ranking list) for more than 10 years (2009–2019). He earned 10 career Overall World Cup titles and recorded 58 individual World Cup victories, ranking him second all-time among men. /3

Davis successfully defended his title in the 2010 Vancouver Winter Olympics, becoming the first man to win back-to-back Olympic gold medals in the 1,000-meter distance. He again earned silver in the 1,500-meter. He is considered one of the most technically proficient skaters in history. /2

Gold favourite Ilia Malinin fell repeatedly in his free skate, dropping from first after the short program to 8th overall. He stood in silence, unable to hide his disappointment, as cameras captured the painful end of his Olympic dream.

#2 Vonn suffers serious injury crash
At 41, Lindsey Vonn returned in 2024 after six years away, chasing one last Olympic moment. After a serious knee injury in training, her downhill ended in a violent crash. She was airlifted off the course—her comeback and Olympic dream ending in heartbreak.

#1 Atle Lie McGrath’s Olympic dream ends in heartbreak
Leading for gold, Norway’s McGrath straddled a gate and DNF’d. Devastated, he threw his poles, climbed off the course, and lay alone in the snow. Skiing in honor of his late grandfather, he later called it the worst moment of his career.
